rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Surcharge, Attorney'S Fees And Costs (These Are The Remaining Issues From Jerome Black'S Petition Filed On 7/12/18)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Please be prepared at the hearing to address whether Dessie Silva has a personal representative if not who are the heirs of Dessie Silva so the court can determine notice. 2) The 12/19/2019 Order Settling 2013 account approved the account and acts except as to $1,070.00 credit card payment described as Miscellaneous. Jerome Black is now alleging an additional $490.00 in unknown purchases for unknown reasons during the 2013 accounting period should be surcharged. Frank Lewis alleges there are $1,560.07 in unexplained expenditures. 3) The 07/28/2020 Order Settling 2014/2015 account was accepted as filed. All acts and transactions of petitioners set forth in the account and report or relating to the matters set forth in it are confirmed and accepted. Jerome Black is now alleging an additional $20,170.00 during the 2014/2015 accounting period should be surcharged. Frank Lewis alleges there is $170.00 in unexplained expenditures during this account period. 4) The 01/25/2021 Order Settling 2016/2017 account allowed the account and accepted the acts and transactions of Petitioners except for the $337.19 in unexplained expenditures and $4,358.00 in unexplained withdrawals. Jerome Black is now alleging an additional $12,618.00 during the 2016/2017 accounting period should be surcharged. Frank Lewis alleges there is $5,435.19 in unexplained expenditures during this account period. 5) The 11/08/2021 Order Settling 2018/2019 account was accepted as filed and the court makes no finding as to surcharge at this time. Frank Lewis alleges there is $4,200.00 in unexplained expenditures during this account period. 6) Jerome Black requests interest at the legal rate on $305,000.00 for the period of 04/09/2019-07/16/2020. 7) Whether $32,821.49 in fees born by the trust as result of the trustee's failure to keep records, failure to produce accounting, failure to keep beneficiaries informed should be surcharged to the former trustees. Frank Lewis alleges Petitioner cannot logically claim the cost of preparing those accounts as damages after the court approved them as reasonable. This claim should be dismissed out of hand. 8) Whether Jerome Black's requested attorney fees of $45,465.00 and $2,790.17 in costs should be paid by Jerome Black, from the trust, or former trustees Dessie Silva and Frank Lewis. Frank Lewis alleges the petitioners actions do not qualify for the common fund theory and Probate Code 16440(a) does not apply.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARILYN SILVERMAN-BALLONOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: a. Conservatee was not given notice of the filing of the first amendment, which was filed on 10/14/21. b. The Notice filed on 10/21/21 was not re-served to better distinguish the two filings made (or to provide notice of only the filing of the 1st amendment filed on 10/14/21). c. Notice to CAA Charles Jonas on filing of the 2nd amendment was provided by email only. d. Petitioner requests approval of fees and costs to litigation attorney Solan Park Robello for period ending 9/3/19 in the sum of $90,676.98 paid per 10/2/18 and 1/22/19 orders. The total amounts listed in the first and second accounts as being paid to Solan Park Robello appear to only amount to $89,676.98. In addition, the $10,000 entry paid on 8/16/18 during the first accounting does not reference any invoices. Also, invoices 613 ($7,557.50) and 678 ($1,505) are attached, but the accounting does not show that they were paid. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/12/2022. Court calendar full. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA P CORPUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/05/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 03/21/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL LEE SIEWERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/23/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: How does the decedent still inherit from his brother when he predeceased him? The COMPLETE title of the petition must be set forth on the face of the Notice of Hearing. In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.] (Letters of Administration, not Testamentary, were issued on 9/17/20. The administrator was appointed and granted IAEA authority on 9/16/20. Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be answered when a Probate Referee WAS appointed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (i.e., a supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the administrator) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/14/22. (If the decedent does not, in fact, inherit from his post-deceased brother, an amendment to the petition must be filed. An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDE L HEATER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees

ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) This third amended account begins 09/14/2020 not at Decedent's date of death 05/28/2020. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. (LRSF 14.34). 2) The third amended petition was not actually processed until 02/17/2022. Notice was given prior to the third amended petition being filed on 02/15/2022. It appears required 15 days' notice should be re-done. 3) The account does not begin with the corrected I&A value of $1,488,000.00 instead begins with $952,926.17 which was the amount on a prior I&A. It appears the account needs to be amended again to begin with $1,488,000.00. 4) The following is a list of individuals the court has for help with court accountings. a. FOR ASSISTANCE WITH COURT ACCOUNTINGS i. Estate Minders (415) 456-8535 ii. Martin Harris (415) 595-6797 iii. Rebecca Hile (707) 254-9966 iv. Julie Kawashima (510) 336-1675 v. Lynn Nightingale (415) 554-0200 vi. Vicci Stillwell (925) 768-6094 5) The West America Bank statement shows the ending balance on 01/26/2022 and does not verify the balance at the end of the account period 01/31/2022. The Wells Fargo bank statements and letter dated 02/11/2022 do not verify the ending balance on 01/31/2022. 6) Counsel provided an unfiled response to Examiner notes in addition to the amended petition. Response to Examiner notes should be incorporated into the amended petition and examiners will not review unfiled documents. (See LRSF 14.7.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BARBARA J BARDARO REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions: Appiontment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/26/2022 by the assigned judicial officer after review. The following issues must be fully addressed before the 04/26/2022 hearing: 1) The settlor only named two professional fiduciaries and one bank as successor trustees. Please explain why the Court should not require appointment of either a professional fiduciary or a financial institution as successor trustee. 2) A proof of service on Christine Witherspoon in the manner required by Probate Code section 17203(b) or her rejection of the trust must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MERCEDES RENDON TRUST DATED OCTOBER 14, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duty/Constructive Fraud; 2. Determination Of Wrongful Taking Of Trust Property And Imposition Of Double Damages; 3. Imposition Of An Equitable Lien; 4. Conversion; 5. Imposition Of Constructive Trust; 6. Removal Of Trustees; And 7. For An Award Of Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/5/22 for corrected proof of service to be filed (or for notice to be mailed along with a copy of the petition, if not already done with the correct Notice of Hearing). The petitioner does not identify the professional fiduciary to be appointed as a successor trustee. Such person must also consent to serve. Courtesy copies of any new filings must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/25/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES EDWARD DOHERTY JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to determine whether sufficient effort was made re the gift to 55 Laguna Open House. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KING MUI CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/22/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of service or proof of publication. No proposed order. Proposed orders must be provided in hard copy and not merely e-filed. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/11/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARTHOLOMEW POOLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: All of Decedent's relatives have not yet been identified. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.
